Cut the graph into two parts such that the number of edges between the cuts is same.
Bisect the network such that the number of nodes on either side of the cut is equal
tool:
def process_graph(graph_data):
    # Convert the directed graph to an undirected graph
    undirected_graph = graph_data.to_undirected()

    # Use spectral partitioning to bisect the networkx graph into two equal halves
    laplacian = nx.normalized_laplacian_matrix(undirected_graph)
    eigvals, eigvecs = np.linalg.eig(laplacian.A)
    partition = eigvecs[:, np.argsort(eigvals)[1]] > 0

    # Assign a unique color to each of the two groups of nodes resulting from the bisection
    for i, node in enumerate(graph_data.nodes()):
        graph_data.nodes[node]['color'] = 'red' if partition[i] else 'blue'

    # Return the modified networkx graph object with the nodes colored based on the bisection
    return_object = {
        'type': 'graph',
        'data': nx.node_link_data(graph_data)
    }
    return return_object
